bullets.text: Net premiums written surged 35% to $2,457.2 million from $1,819.2 million in 1993, with Core business NPW up 38% to $2,341 million; 1994 NPW growth of $638 million exceeded total company NPW in 1985.
Underwriting profit margin was 8.3% in 1994 (11.5% including elimination of the $71 million supplemental reserve); ROE was 27.4%, down from 36.0% as margins moderated toward the 4% long-term target.
Underwriting expense ratio declined to 24% of premiums in 1994 from 26% in 1993 and 31% in 1989, driven by TQM, process management, and steady cost reduction initiatives.
Investment portfolio grew to $3,180.0 million; $200 million of 6.60% 10-year notes issued in January 1994; 1,080,300 Common Shares repurchased at an average cost of $31.44 per share.

Progressive expanded multi-distribution (agent, telephone, office, mail) across Florida, Texas, and Ohio, with independent agents still accounting for over 90% of 1994 volume, while standard/preferred market grew to 5-10% of total volume.
Progressive reached 7th largest U.S. private passenger auto insurer with 2.2% market share, competing directly with State Farm, Allstate, Farmers, USAA, Nationwide, GEICO, and Safeco.

Progressive consolidated new customer services into an emerging brand, producing TV commercials highlighting Express Quote service and testing multi-channel consumer advertising in Florida, Texas, and Ohio.

Progressive continued learning to price the 80%+ standard and preferred market; limited standard/preferred volume to 5-10% of total while adjusting rates immediately based on emerging loss experience.

Management framed long-term strategy around building intrinsic value via a growing customer base and multi-channel brand experience, with shareholder returns measured over five-year periods rather than quarterly.
